questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani is recognised as the first woman to hold which office in India?
questions.explanation: Sucheta Kripalani was the first woman to become a Chief Minister of an Indian state.

questions.q: Of which Indian state was Sucheta Kripalani the Chief Minister?
questions.explanation: She headed the government of Uttar Pradesh as Chief Minister.

questions.q: In which year did Sucheta Kripalani become Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: She took office as Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh in 1963.

questions.q: Until which year did Sucheta Kripalani serve as Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Her term as Chief Minister ran from 1963 to 1967.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani was an active participant in which body that drafted India's Constitution?
questions.explanation: She was an active participant in the Constituent Assembly of India.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani was the first woman to lead a state government as its head of government, a post known as the:
questions.explanation: The head of a state government in India is the Chief Minister, the office she first held among women.

questions.q: The 'first woman Chief Minister' distinction of Sucheta Kripalani relates to government at which level?
questions.explanation: A Chief Minister heads the government at the state level.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani's career as a Chief Minister was associated with which decade?
questions.explanation: She served as Chief Minister from 1963 to 1967, during the 1960s.

questions.q: Before independence, the body in which Sucheta Kripalani participated was tasked with which function?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly, in which she took part, drafted the Constitution of India.

questions.q: Uttar Pradesh, the state Sucheta Kripalani led, is located in which broad region of India?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh is a large state in northern India.

questions.q: For how many years, approximately, did Sucheta Kripalani serve as Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Her tenure from 1963 to 1967 lasted about four years.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani took office as Chief Minister in October 1963, while which person was still serving as Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: When she became Chief Minister in October 1963, Jawaharlal Nehru was still Prime Minister; he held the office until his death in May 1964.

questions.q: The Constituent Assembly, in which Sucheta Kripalani participated, was presided over by which person?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad served as President of the Constituent Assembly in which she took part.

questions.q: As Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh, Sucheta Kripalani was the constitutional head of which kind of body?
options: The Council of Ministers of a state
questions.explanation: A Chief Minister heads the Council of Ministers of a state government.

questions.q: The office of Chief Minister that Sucheta Kripalani held is, in a state, broadly analogous to which Union office?
questions.explanation: A Chief Minister is the head of government in a state, analogous to the Prime Minister at the Union level.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani's distinction as first woman Chief Minister came before India had its first woman in which other high office?
questions.explanation: She became the first woman Chief Minister in 1963, before India had its first woman Prime Minister in 1966.

questions.q: Membership in the Constituent Assembly, as held by Sucheta Kripalani, placed her among the framers of which document?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly framed the Constitution of India.

questions.q: The state government Sucheta Kripalani led as Chief Minister was based in the capital of which state?
questions.explanation: She led the government of Uttar Pradesh, whose administration she headed as Chief Minister.

questions.q: A Chief Minister such as Sucheta Kripalani is appointed by which constitutional functionary of a state?
questions.explanation: The Governor of a state appoints the Chief Minister, who heads the state government.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani's role in the Constituent Assembly took place during which broad period?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly drafted the Constitution in the late 1940s, when she participated.

questions.q: Which statement best summarises Sucheta Kripalani's place in Indian political history?
options: First woman Chief Minister of an Indian state
First woman Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: She is recognised as the first woman to become a Chief Minister of an Indian state.

questions.q: As Chief Minister, Sucheta Kripalani would have been collectively responsible to which body?
questions.explanation: A state Council of Ministers, headed by the Chief Minister, is collectively responsible to the State Legislative Assembly.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani became Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh in the same year that India fought a border conflict with which neighbour the previous year?
questions.explanation: She took office in 1963, the year after the 1962 India-China border conflict.

questions.q: The directly elected lawmaking body of Uttar Pradesh, before which Sucheta Kripalani's ministry was answerable, is known as the:
questions.explanation: The Vidhan Sabha is the Legislative Assembly of a state, to which the Chief Minister's ministry is answerable.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani's term as Chief Minister ended in 1967, the same year as which recurring national exercise?
questions.explanation: Her term ended in 1967, a year in which general elections to the Lok Sabha and state assemblies were held.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani's husband, J.B. Kripalani, was a noted figure associated chiefly with which sphere?
questions.explanation: J.B. Kripalani, her husband, was a well-known figure in Indian public and political life.

questions.q: Before leading the Uttar Pradesh government, Sucheta Kripalani had also served as a member of which national legislative body?
questions.explanation: She served as a member of the Lok Sabha, India's directly elected lower house, during her national-level career.

questions.q: Sucheta Kripalani's tenure as Chief Minister fell entirely within which decade of the Indian Republic?
questions.explanation: Her 1963-1967 term lay wholly within the second decade of the Republic, the 1960s.

questions.q: As a Constituent Assembly participant, Sucheta Kripalani is documented as having sung a patriotic song at which historic sitting?
options: The first Lok Sabha sitting in 1952
questions.explanation: She is recorded as having sung at the Constituent Assembly's session held on the eve of independence in August 1947.

questions.q: The milestone Sucheta Kripalani set in 1963 concerns women's representation specifically at the apex of which type of executive?
questions.explanation: She became the first woman to head a state executive as Chief Minister.
